You are working in /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service (NestJS/TypeScript monorepo).

## Your Task
Fix ESLint `custom-rules/require-object-params` violations for Jira ticket RECO-693.
Ticket URL: https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-693

## Context
- Current branch: `RECO-691-fix-utils-require-object-params`
- You must create a NEW branch from this branch (NOT from main)
- Branch name format: `RECO-693-<kebab-summary>`
- Commit format: `RECO-693: <imperative description>`

## ESLint Rule
`custom-rules/require-object-params` — all exported functions must use a single object destructuring parameter instead of positional params.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
// ❌ Before
export function myFn(id: string, name: string): void {}
// ✅ After
export function myFn({ id, name }: { id: string; name: string }): void {}
```

## Steps

### 1. Find the target file
Run ESLint to find remaining files with violations:
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 src/ --format=compact 2>&1 | grep "require-object-params" | head -40
```
Pick an appropriate unfixed file for RECO-693. Focus on files in `src/utils/`, `src/modules/`, or `src/scripts/`.

### 2. Create branch
```bash
git checkout -b RECO-693-<descriptive-name>
```
Branch from current branch `RECO-691-fix-utils-require-object-params`.

### 3. Fix violations in target file(s)
- Change positional params to `{ param }: { param: type }` destructuring
- Update ALL internal calls to changed functions
- Find ALL callers: use Grep tool to search for function names across src/ and test/
- Update every call site
- Do NOT use `{ ...param }` spread shortcuts

### 4. Update tests
Find and update all test files that call changed functions.

### 5. Run tests
```bash
npm run test:unit -- --testPathPatterns="<pattern>" 2>&1
```

### 6. Run type-check
```bash
npm run type-check 2>&1
```

### 7. Verify ESLint
```bash
npx eslint --max-warnings=0 <changed-files> 2>&1
```

### 8. Commit (only relevant files — NOT .husky/pre-push)
```bash
git add <specific-files-only>
git commit -m "RECO-693: <description>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

### 9. Push
```bash
git push -u origin RECO-693-<branch-name>
```

### 10. Create DRAFT PR
Base = `RECO-691-fix-utils-require-object-params` (NOT main).

```bash
gh pr create --draft --base RECO-691-fix-utils-require-object-params --title "RECO-693: ..." --body "$(cat <<'EOF'

JIRA task: [RECO-693](https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-693)
Design: N/A

### Changes 🍍

<describe changes>

### PR Checks 🐊

- [x] I've self-reviewed my code.
- [x] I've added the necessary labels.
- [x] I've added tests for my code.
- [ ] I've updated the documentation.

### Description / Ticket Summary 🐙

Fix ESLint custom-rules/require-object-params violations — refactored functions to use single object destructuring parameter pattern.

### Screenshots / Short Videos 🐮

N/A
EOF
)"
```

## Critical Rules
- NEVER modify `.husky/pre-push`
- NEVER use relative imports (use `@utils/`, `@modules/`, etc.)
- NO comments in code
- All functions need explicit return types
- Do NOT use `{ ...spread }` as shortcut

2. The PR URL
3. Summary of files changed

Now I understand the full picture:
1. `style-colors.service.ts` line 13: `storeStyle(data)` - needs to become `storeStyle({ data }: { data: ... })`
2. `style-color.model.ts` line 222: `upsertStyle(data)` - needs to become `upsertStyle({ data }: { data: ... })`
3. `style-color.model.ts` line 352: `parseStyleColorData(data)` - needs to become `parseStyleColorData({ data }: { data: ... })` - BUT this is a `private` method. Let me check if the rule applies to private methods.

The rule says "Custom functions must use a single object destructuring parameter" - let me check if private methods are exempt. Let me look at the ESLint rule:

content.text:
The rule applies to all methods (including private), but NOT framework methods or constructors. I see the issue clearly now:
- The rule applies to ALL methods including private ones
- For `parseStyleColorData(data: IStyleColorBase)` at line 352, it needs to become `parseStyleColorData({ data }: { data: IStyleColorBase })`
- For `upsertStyle(data: ...)` at line 222, it needs to become `upsertStyle({ data }: { data: ... })`
- For `storeStyle(data: ...)` in service at line 13, it needs to become `storeStyle({ data }: { data: ... })`

Now let me check how `style-colors.service.ts` `storeStyle` is called and how `model.upsertStyle` is called from the service:

Looking at the code:
- `storeStyle.service.ts` line 13: `storeStyle(data: ...)` needs to wrap as `{ data }`
